Interface Image

All Known Subinterfaces:
RasterImage, VectorImage
All Known Implementing Classes:
AWTRasterImage, SVGImage
public sealed interface Image permits RasterImage, VectorImage
An immutable image that can be rendered on a canvas.

This interface abstracts over various image formats (raster, vector, procedural) and enables uniform handling across rendering pipelines. Implementations may represent:

  • Raster images (PNG, JPEG)
  • Vector graphics (SVG)
  • Dynamic/procedural textures

Resource Management

Images are lazy: they do not load their data until prepare() is called. This allows efficient handling of large or rarely used assets.

The image itself does not manage GPU resources (like textures) — that is the responsibility of the graphics engine. Therefore, implementations should not hold native GPU memory.

Some images, however, may hold native non-GPU memory, so they have to be unloaded manually once no longer used.

Lifecycle

It's best to load images through GraphicsManager's methods for automatic resource management. If there's a need to do it manually, though, here's how:
  1. Create image (e.g., from file or stream)
  2. Call prepare() before drawing
  3. Draw via RenderCanvas.drawImage(Image, double, double, double, double)
  4. Call unload() when no longer needed

Thread Safety

Implementations must be safe to pass between threads, but are not required to support concurrent drawing from multiple canvases unless explicitly documented.
See Also:
  • RasterImage
  • VectorImage
  • GraphicsManager.loadImage(Path, boolean)
  • GraphicsManager.getLoadedImage(Path)
  • GraphicsManager.hasLoadedImage(Path)
  • GraphicsManager.destroyImage(Path)

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    double
    getIntrinsicHeight()
    Returns the intrinsic height of this image.
    double
    getIntrinsicWidth()
    Returns the intrinsic width of this image.
    default String
    getMimeType()
    Optional hint about the MIME type of this image.
    boolean
    isReady()
    Checks whether this image is ready to be drawn.
    void
    prepare()
    Prepares the image for rendering.
    void
    unload()
    Unloads internal data associated with this image.

  • Method Details

    • prepare

      void prepare()
      Prepares the image for rendering.

      This may involve:

      • Lazy decoding of compressed data
      • Rasterization of vector content
      etc. Once the image is done preparing:
      • isReady() returns true
      • unload() may be used to unload the data

      Idempotent: calling multiple times has no additional effect.

    • isReady

      boolean isReady()
      Checks whether this image is ready to be drawn.

      Returns false only if:

      • The image is still loading asynchronously
      • Required resources failed to load
      • prepare() has never been called or hasn't been called after unloading
      • The image has been previously unloaded with unload()
      Returns:
      true if safe to pass to RenderCanvas.drawImage()

    • unload

      void unload()
      Unloads internal data associated with this image.

      After calling this method:

      • isReady() returns false
      • Subsequent calls to prepare() may reload the data

      Idempotent: calling multiple times has no effect.

    • getIntrinsicWidth

      double getIntrinsicWidth()
      Returns the intrinsic width of this image.

      Intrinsic Size

       Intrinsic size refers to the source's original dimensions. The actual dimensions are able to change depending on scaling and resolution. Examples:
      • Raster images have a fixed size and their intrinsic size is equal to that size;
      • Vector images have a dynamic resolution due to vector shapes being defined as paths, but the intrinsic size is typically set (e.g. <svg> element has attributes "width" & "height").
      Returns:
      the intrinsic width of this image
      See Also:
      • getIntrinsicHeight()

    • getIntrinsicHeight

      double getIntrinsicHeight()
      Returns the intrinsic height of this image. See the definition of intrinsic size.
      Returns:
      the intrinsic height of this image
      See Also:
      • getIntrinsicWidth()

    • getMimeType

      default String getMimeType()
      Optional hint about the MIME type of this image.

      Examples: "image/png", "image/svg+xml"

      May return null if unknown or irrelevant (e.g. procedural images).

      Returns:
      the MIME type, or null if unavailable
